- Apple
- Withings
- Garmin
- Oura Ring
- Fitbit
- Whoop

**Pricing, Release Dates, or Financial Numbers:**
- No specific pricing information is mentioned.
- Samsung received De Novo authorization from the FDA in 2024 for its Sleep Apnea feature.
- Apple received FDA clearance (510k) for its sleep apnea notification feature some months after Samsung.

**Product Specifications, Hardware Details, or Software Features:**
- Apple's sleep apnea notifications are available on Apple Watch Series 9 and later, Apple Watch Ultra 2 and 3, and Apple Watch SE 3.
- Samsung's sleep apnea feature works with the Galaxy Watch 4 and newer.
- Withings' Scan Watch received FDA clearance for tracking breathing disturbances in 2021.
- Oura Ring added a feature in 2024 that flags breathing disruptions during the night.
- Garmin's watches log breathing variations during sleep, but the feature doesn't have FDA clearance.
- Fitbit and Whoop offer advanced sleep-tracking and recovery metrics, including nighttime respiratory rate tracking, but no FDA-cleared respiratory feature.

**Direct Quotes:**
- None

**Additional Facts:**
- Sleep apnea is a serious disorder where breathing repeatedly stops or is disrupted while sleeping.
- Confirming a diagnosis of sleep apnea requires medical testing.
- Some smartwatches can screen for signs that may suggest sleep apnea, and users can visit their doctor to get a formal diagnosis.
- The feature requires users to set up sleep tracking and log their sleep for at least 10 nights within a 30-day period before receiving notifications.

title: IBM has unveiled chip technology that could help extend Moore’s Law another decade
summary:
Here are the crucial facts extracted from the raw text:

* **Core Story/Main Event:**
 + IBM has unveiled a new chip technology called "nanostack" that vertically stacks transistors in two layers on a silicon chip, potentially extending Moore's Law for another decade.
* **Important Names, Companies, or People:**
 + Jay Gambetta, Director of IBM Research
 + Dan Hutcheson, Vice Chair of Tech Insights
 + Huiming Bu, IBM's Vice President of Global Semiconductor R&D
 + Qing Cao, Professor of Materials Science and Engineering at the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign
 + IBM
 + Intel
 + Samsung
 + TSMC
 + Imec (Belgian research lab)
 + AMD
 + Huawei
* **Pricing, Release Dates, or Financial Numbers:**
 + No specific pricing or financial numbers mentioned
 + IBM expects chips with nanostacking to be widely used in data centers within a decade
* **Product Specifications, Hardware Details, or Software Features:**
 + The new chip has around 100 billion transistors on an area the size of a fingernail, twice the density of IBM's previous state-of-the-art technology
 + The chip uses a nanostack architecture, vertically stacking transistors in two layers on a silicon chip
 + The transistors in the second layer are staggered, simplifying wiring and other advantages
 + The chip can do as much as 50% more work in the same amount of time and be up to 70% more energy efficient compared to IBM's previous state-of-the-art architecture
 + The chip uses a complementary field-effect transistor (CFET) design
* **Direct Quotes:**
 + "It's not just an incremental step, it's a meaningful leap forward." - Jay Gambetta, Director of IBM Research
 + "Absolutely, it's transformational. This puts another 10, 15 years on the roadmap." - Dan Hutcheson, Vice Chair of Tech Insights
 + "I expect to have many conversations with designers about how they can use this technology." - Huiming Bu, IBM's Vice President of Global Semiconductor R&D
 + "I'm interested in what's their killer application." - Qing Cao, Professor of Materials Science and Engineering at the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign

title: macOS Golden Gate Beta 2 Now available for Developers
summary: Apple today provided developers with the second beta of mac OS Golden Gate , with the update coming two weeks after Apple seeded the first beta. Developers can download ‌mac OS Golden Gate‌ by going to System Settings > General > Software Update. Beta Updates will need to be enabled, and a free developer account is required. ‌mac OS Golden Gate‌ includes multiple design improvements. Liquid Glass opacity has been improved to increase readability, there's a slider for controlling the level of transparency, and changes to shadowing make it easier to tell when a window is active. Apps now have uniform toolbars, edge-to-edge sidebars, and less rounded corners, plus there are fewer icons in menu bars. Siri has been transformed into ‌Siri‌ AI, a chatbot-style assistant that combines general world knowledge, your personal data, and onscreen awareness to answer questions about anything and find information for you. ‌Siri‌ lives in Spotlight, and you can ask questions with the new Search or Ask interface. There's also a ‌Siri‌ app where you can chat with ‌Siri‌ and view past conversations, and on newer Macs, you can set a custom voice for ‌Siri‌ with personalized pace and expressivity. Visual Intelligence is now on the Mac so ‌Siri‌ can answer questions about what's on your screen, and a Write with ‌Siri‌ feature lets ‌Siri‌ generate text or give feedback on your writing. There are AI photo editing tools in the Photos app, AI tab organization in Safari, an option to use AI to automatically update weak passwords in the Passwords app, and a revamped Image Playground app that can generate photorealistic images. More on all of the new features in ‌mac OS Golden Gate‌ can be found in our mac OS Golden Gate roundup . ‌mac OS Golden Gate‌ is limited to developers right now, but Apple plans to release a public beta in July. Related Roundup: mac OS Golden Gate This article, " mac OS Golden Gate Beta 2 Now available for Developers " first appeared on Mac Rumors.com Discuss this article in our forums

title: Apple Seeds iOS 27 and iPadOS 27 Second Betas to Developers
summary: Apple today seeded the second betas of i OS 27 and i Pad OS 27 to developers for testing purposes, with the update coming two weeks after Apple released the first betas following the WWDC 2026 keynote. Registered developers can download the betas from the Settings app on the i Phone or i Pad by going to the General section and selecting Software Update. ‌i OS 27‌ introduces Apple's smarter version of Siri , ‌Siri‌ AI. ‌Siri‌ AI is a chatbot similar to Chat GPT, incorporating general world knowledge, personal data from apps like Messages and Mail, and onscreen awareness to answer questions and find information for you. There are new Apple Intelligence features in apps like Photos , Safari, Shortcuts, Wallet, and Passwords, plus Apple added a standalone ‌Siri‌ app that houses ‌Siri‌ conversations. Write with ‌Siri‌ is available systemwide for generating text or offering feedback on your writing, and Visual Intelligence has been relocated to the Camera app with a new ‌Siri‌ mode. Apple improved the look of Liquid Glass and added a slider for customizing transparency. There are multiple performance improvements to speed up i OS, so everything feels faster, even on older i Phones. Apps launch quicker, Air Drop transfers are faster, the keyboard pops up more quickly, and devices are better at transitioning between Wi-Fi and Cellular to keep you connected. For more on what's new in ‌i OS 27‌, we have a dedicated i OS 27 roundup . Related Roundups: i OS 27 , i Pad OS 27 This article, " Apple Seeds i OS 27 and i Pad OS 27 Second Betas to Developers " first appeared on Mac Rumors.com Discuss this article in our forums

title: Advanced AI Dictation Not Enabled by Default in iOS 27 Beta
summary: Apple's next-generation AI dictation feature for the i Phone 17 Pro and i Phone Air is not turned on by default in the first developer beta of i OS 27 . Apple says the new AI-powered dictation system delivers "a major boost in accuracy," with more reliable on-the-fly capitalization and punctuation than the existing dictation system. The feature runs on Apple's new AFM 3 Core Advanced model , which is a 20-billion-parameter, natively multimodal system that uses a sparse architecture, activating just one to four billion parameters at a time depending on the request. To fit a model that large onto a smartphone, the full model is stored in flash memory rather than DRAM, with a lightweight routing block selecting a fixed set of "experts" during initial processing and periodically reselecting them during generation, a technique Apple calls Instruction-Following Pruning. In side-by-side human evaluations against Apple's previous production dictation system across seven quality dimensions, AFM 3 Core Advanced was preferred on overall quality by a margin of 44.7% to 17.6%, with that preference holding consistently across the other six dimensions, which include punctuation, casing, layout, meaning capture, disfluency handling, and style. Because of the model's size, the upgraded dictation is limited to a handful of newer devices: the ‌i Phone 17 Pro‌ and ‌i Phone 17 Pro‌ Max, the ‌i Phone Air‌, the Vision Pro with M5 chip, i Pads with an M4 chip or later with at least 12GB of RAM, and Macs with an M3 chip or later with at least 12GB of RAM. Notably, the standard i Phone 17 is excluded, as it ships with 8GB of RAM rather than the 12GB the larger model requires. The same AFM Core Advanced model also powers Apple's new customizable expressive Siri voices , another opt-in preview as of beta 1. The new dictation model runs entirely on-device, so transcription quality stays the same whether or not the i Phone is connected to a network. It remains unclear whether the preview will stay off by default when ‌i OS 27‌ is released officially later this year, or whether Apple will switch it on automatically at some point during the beta cycle this summer. Related Roundups: i OS 27 , i Pad OS 27 Tag: Apple Intelligence This article, " Advanced AI Dictation Not Enabled by Default in i OS 27 Beta " first appeared on Mac Rumors.com Discuss this article in our forums

title: Penalty Shootouts: Is the Team That Kicks First More Likely to Win?
summary:
Here's a highly condensed bulleted list of crucial facts extracted from the raw text:

* **Core Story/Main Event:** Penalty shootouts in soccer, specifically the debate on whether the team that kicks first has an advantage in winning.
* **Important Names, Companies, or People:**
	+ Jorge Garay (contributor to WIRED en Español)
	+ WIRED (media outlet)
	+ FIFA (Fédération Internationale de Football Association)
* **Pricing, Release Dates, or Financial Numbers:** None mentioned in the text.
* **Product Specifications, Hardware Details, or Software Features:** None mentioned in the text.
* **Direct Quotes:**
	+ "The apparent advantage of the first team does not stem from the order of the kicks but rather from the type of psychological situations that order creates." (authors of the study)
* **Key Findings:**
	+ A study published in 2010 found that teams that started the shootout won nearly 60% of the time, compared to 40% for those who took their penalty kicks second.
	+ Subsequent studies have reduced the estimated size of the advantage, with one study finding no evidence that the team taking the first shot wins more often than the team taking the second.
	+ A recent study found that penalty kicks where a goal immediately secured victory were successful 89.1% of the time, while those where a miss meant immediate elimination had a success rate of 60.4%.
	+ The study suggests that the apparent advantage of the first team may be due to the type of psychological situations created by the order of the kicks, rather than the order itself.

- Anthropic: company that developed Claude Opus 4.7 and has a Cyber Verification Program for security researchers
- Front Gate Tickets: ticketing company that handles events for major US music festivals
- Live Nation Entertainment: parent company of Front Gate Tickets
- Peter Thiel: co-founder of Dialog, a private events group

**Pricing, Release Dates, or Financial Numbers:**
- $4,000: the value of a ticket that Carroll could issue freely
- Millions: the number of customer or staff records that the vulnerability provided access to

**Product Specifications, Hardware Details, or Software Features:**
- Claude Opus 4.7: an AI tool that can assist in finding web vulnerabilities
- SQL injection vulnerability: a common flaw that allows a hacker to input commands into a text field on a website
- Nested SQL query: a technique used by Claude to evade the firewall's detection
- Web application firewall: a security control that was bypassed by Claude

**Direct Quotes:**
- "It was pretty cool to see a ticket that's $4,000, and I could just hit a button and issue as many as I wanted." - Ian Carroll
- "It just feels concerning when you think these very professional music festivals with professional websites are well-run. Then you get access, and you realize it's all held together by duct tape and prayers." - Ian Carroll

* **Important Names, Companies, or People:**

 + "The challenge is finding a way to unlock AI's enormous benefits while preventing its growing risks."
* **Other Key Facts:**
 + The report highlights the rapid evolution of AI capabilities, with tasks doubling every few months.
 + AI has massive benefits for humanity, including accelerating drug discovery, vaccine development, and early detection of illnesses.
 + AI has created new kinds of harms, including the generation and distribution of deepfakes and false information.
 + Policymakers struggle to keep pace with AI development due to the technology's rapid evolution and the need for scientific data before introducing regulations.
 + The UN panel recommends stronger independent evaluation, international cooperation, and common standards to ensure AI systems remain safe, transparent, and accountable.
 + Access to AI systems is heavily concentrated in developed countries, with most systems developed in the US and China.
 + Most developing countries lack the infrastructure and expertise needed to benefit from AI truly.

title: Apple Hide My Email Vulnerability Exposes Real Email Addresses
summary:
Here's a highly condensed bulleted list of crucial facts extracted from the raw text:

* **Core Story/Main Event:**
 + A vulnerability in Apple's Hide My Email service allows almost anyone to uncover the real email address behind a generated alias.
* **Important Names, Companies, or People:**
 + Tyler Murphy, co-founder of Easy Opt Outs, discovered the issue and responsibly reported it to Apple.
 + Apple (company)
* **Pricing, Release Dates, or Financial Numbers:**
 + None mentioned in the text
* **Product Specifications, Hardware Details, or Software Features:**
 + Hide My Email is an iCloud+ feature that generates random alias email addresses to protect a user's real email address from spam, data breaches, and unwanted identification.
 + The feature is available on a dedicated "private.icloud.com" domain.
* **Direct Quotes:**
 + "Apple Hide My Email is leaking email addresses that are supposed to be hidden. We reported the issue and replication instructions to Apple over a year ago. We don't know why it hasn't been fixed, but we don't feel comfortable waiting any longer." - Tyler Murphy
 + (No second direct quote is present in the text)
* **Timeline:**
 + June 2025: Tyler Murphy reported the issue to Apple.
 + July 2025: Apple acknowledged the report and said it was investigating.
 + March 2026: Apple told Murphy it had "addressed the reported issue in a recent system change."
 + May 2026: Apple said the issue remained under investigation and asked Murphy not to disclose it publicly until the inquiry was complete.
 + End of May 2026: Apple said it expected to address the issue in a security update "expected in the coming weeks."

title: LLMs are stuck in a groupthink groove. This startup is trying to get them out.
summary:
Here's a highly condensed bulleted list of crucial facts extracted from the text:

**Core Story / Main Event:**
- Large language models (LLMs) are stuck in a groupthink groove, producing predictable and less creative responses.
- Springboards, an Australian startup, has developed an LLM called Flint to address this issue.

**Important Names, Companies, or People:**
- Pip Bingemann, cofounder and CEO of Springboards
- Kieran Browne, cofounder and CTO of Springboards
- Zoe Scaman, founder of Bodacious and chief strategy officer at 77X
- Luka Dončić, founder of 77X
- Maximilian Weigl, cofounder and chief strategy officer at Uncommon
- Kieran Browne, cofounder and CTO of Springboards
- Open AI (parent company of Chat GPT)
- Alibaba (parent company of Qwen 3)

**Pricing, Release Dates, or Financial Numbers:**
- No specific pricing or financial numbers mentioned in the text.

**Product Specifications, Hardware Details, or Software Features:**
- Flint is an LLM trained on top of Qwen 3, an open-source model from Alibaba.
- Flint is designed to produce a wider variety of responses by identifying points in its output where more variety is possible and filling those spots with words or phrases that are a little more random.
- The tool developed by Springboards allows users to drag around text produced by different models, picking the bits that they like and combining them into something new.

**Direct Quotes:**
- "Most language models are fighting hallucinations. We welcome them." - Pip Bingemann
- "There's all this lost information that doesn't get served up in these models. They're just as capable of saying a Buick or a Tesla. They just don't—they're biased." - Pip Bingemann
- "I find it really useful for throwing me in completely different directions. I use it if I want to catapult myself all over the place." - Zoe Scaman
- "That was really interesting. It sometimes falls over when you start pushing it too far. But I think that the premise behind it is really powerful." - Zoe Scaman
- "Variety is great when you're trying to spark ideas. Let's go down this route instead of letting the machines do it all and ending up in a gray, boring world." - Pip Bingemann

  - Supports the World Cup games currently underway in the United States.

title: Yep, we’re using OpenClaw to date now
summary:

**Important Names, Companies, or People:**
- Ben Guez (content creator and startup founder)
- Amanda Silberling (Tech Crunch senior writer)
- Jeff Weisbein (founder of a tech PR firm)
- Cailey (tech worker)
- Lazer Cohen (co-founder of Nano Claw)
- Isabel J. Kim (science fiction author and co-host of Wow If True podcast)
- Tim Fernholz (author of "The 'Father of the Internet' is finally retiring" article)

**Pricing, Release Dates, or Financial Numbers:**
- No specific pricing or financial numbers mentioned in the text.

**Product Specifications, Hardware Details, or Software Features:**
- Open Claw: an open-source AI agent that can be used for various tasks, including automating Instagram posts.
- Claude: a conversational AI tool that can be used to create automated messages and posts.
- Nano Claw: a personal AI assistant that supports agent swarms and has a focus on security and human-in-the-loop approval.
- Rosie (Nano Claw assistant): used by Lazer Cohen and his wife to manage their children's schedules.

**Direct Quotes:**
- "I think it's crazy, like the potential is insane right now." - Ben Guez
- "I'm not sure if everyone's gonna think it's good, but I mean, it's working." - Ben Guez
- "They're not feeling angry, they're more impressed, like, 'Oh, you're thinking outside of the box, you're a genius.'" - Ben Guez
- "I think as long as you're open [about] what you're doing, I think it's fine." - Ben Guez
- "I'm meeting women who are in various parts of South Florida, so I don't know all of the restaurants or things to do... I have my bot just kind of do all the research and make a document with links to why it's a choice for whatever type of date it is." - Jeff Weisbein
- "I guess I'm not leveraging Open Claw to the fullest... But definitely in the realm of using Open Claw to facilitate a task that I would manually have to do otherwise." - Jeff Weisbein
- "I have seen people create bots and ways to swipe using Open Claw, and I wouldn't do that. They say it's a numbers game, but if that's what it takes... that seems like a pretty terrible way to do it." - Jeff Weisbein
- "I feel like you shouldn't delegate your communication when you're in a relationship with someone to AI." - Jeff Weisbein
- "I started using Claude and created an automation that crafts 'I no longer wish to see you' messages based on a few key terms I would enter about the date. It'd then automatically send them for me at random times so that I wouldn't feel the anxiety of when to send." - Cailey
- "Whenever you're giving an agent access to personal information and accounts, you need human-in-the-loop approval." - Lazer Cohen
- "We've all heard the stories of Open Claw creating dating profiles for people without their knowledge or consent, or Open Claw dating coaches spilling to other groups that they're being used as a dating coach too." - Lazer Cohen
- "My wife and I personally use our Nano Claw assistant, Rosie, to manage the schedules of our five children... But 'claws' are widely used to help couples get to the child-rearing phase." - Lazer Cohen

**Important Names, Companies, or People:**

- "I don’t think that’s a surprise to anyone in this room. Everywhere here at Cannes, the conversation is about AI."
- "It’s crazy. I’ve been in this business for a long time and we are seeing all kinds of partners offer wild things in the pitch process."
- "The AI story is the new programmatic story, with the promise of everything just being absolutely automated, and that absolutely did not happen."

title: I Tried Rips, the Card-Pack App Where Users Spend Thousands Chasing Pricey Pokémon
summary:
Here's the extracted information in a clean, highly condensed bulleted list:

**Core Story/Main Event:**
- The article discusses the author's experience with the Rips app, a digital card-pack app where users spend real money to open packs and win physical cards, often with the goal of selling them back for a profit.
- The app has gained popularity, with over 6 million downloads since its launch in October 2025, and users can spend thousands of dollars chasing rare and valuable cards.

**Important Names, Companies, or People:**
- Reece Rogers (author of the article and WIRED's service writer)
- Timothy Fong (associate clinical professor of psychiatry at UCLA and codirector of the school's gambling studies program)
- Shane Kraus (expert in gambling-related harms and director of the Behavioral Addictions Lab at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas)
- Logan Paul (influencer who offers a similar format with his app Rip It)
- Nintendo (not involved with Rips, but mentioned as the company behind Pokémon trading cards)

**Pricing, Release Dates, or Financial Numbers:**
- The cheapest option is the Pokémon Starter Pack, which costs $1
- The most expensive pack is the Pokémon Diamond Pack, which costs $2,500
- Users can spend up to $1,000 on a single pack
- The app has generated over $6 million in revenue since its launch in October 2025
- The author spent $20 and turned it into $101 before losing it all
- A user shared a video where they pulled a Pokémon card worth $5,498 out of a $1,000 pack

**Product Specifications, Hardware Details, or Software Features:**
- The app offers three types of cards: Pokémon, Basketball, and One Piece
- Users can change their "volatility level" in the settings to increase their odds of pulling rare cards
- The app has a "Showroom" feature that displays the most expensive, rare cards users can pull from each pack
- Users can instantly sell back their cards for a profit
- The app has a "Responsible Purchasing Policy" that acknowledges the potential for compulsive spending behavior

**Direct Quotes:**
- "As kids, we didn't go with $500 or $1,000 into comic book shops. Here, you certainly can, all under the premise that if I keep hanging on, then I'm going to open something that's so valuable it's gonna break the bank," - Timothy Fong
- "When it says 'slice the seal,' there's an illusion of control that you're doing something. But you're not doing anything." - Timothy Fong
- "It's really attractive looking. It immediately reminded me of a slot machine." - Shane Kraus
- "Everything they did was to manipulate you." - Shane Kraus
- "In the old days, I would take that card and sell it to a human behind the counter. He gives me $50 or says no. Gamesmanship is actually the part of collecting that a lot of people like, because there's a human connection. This has zero human connection. There's no one you're talking to. So, that's the gaming and slot-machine-like design to pull you into a world of what I call dopamine isolation." - Timothy Fong

* **Important Names, Companies, or People:**

 + Apple
* **Pricing, Release Dates, or Financial Numbers:**
 + Opera browser is available as a free update.
 + No specific financial numbers mentioned.
* **Product Specifications, Hardware Details, or Software Features:**
 + Paste Protect feature:
  - Combines existing clipboard hijack protection with a new injection protection system.
  - Monitors clipboard activity for suspicious commands copied from websites.
  - Blocks potentially malicious content before it reaches the clipboard.
  - Users can see the first 120 characters of the blocked content.

* **Additional Facts:**
 + Click Fix attacks accounted for more than 53% of malware-loading cyberattacks last year, according to Huntress research.
 + Apple introduced a related safeguard for the Mac with the release of macOS 26.4 (Tahoe).

title: Apple Ramps Foldable iPhone 'Ultra' Production to 10 Million Units
summary: Apple has told suppliers to prepare to make approximately 10 million foldable i Phones this year, up from a previous forecast of about 7-8 million units a few months ago, reports Nikkei Asia ($). Apple has already booked parts for roughly 80 million smartphones for the second half of 2026, which includes the i Phone 18 Pro, i Phone 18 Pro Max, and the first-ever foldable i Phone. The company's full 2026 production is expected to top 220 million units, according to the publication. Apple's purchasing power is said to have left it better positioned than rivals like Xiaomi, Oppo, and Vivo, which have each cut annual production targets below 100 million units amid an industry-wide memory shortage. Some suppliers have reportedly been told to expect orders for as many as 85 million new i Phones in the second half of 2026, with Apple asking them to reserve i Phone 17 components for the coming i Phone 18 lineup. Engineering problems tied to the foldable i Phone's hinge appear to have been resolved , but that has raised the odds of a small initial shipment following the device's launch. A larger production run likely won't begin until closer to the end of the year. Apple raised prices on Mac Books and i Pads last month in response to rising component costs, but the i Phone 17 lineup has so far been spared from a price hike. If that remains the case, Apple will likely use the new devices launches to introduce increased pricing across the lineup. IDC has predicted that the foldable will carry an average selling price of $2,500, with storage options potentially priced as high as $3,000. Apple's foldable i Phone is rumored to feature a 7.8-inch inner display and a 5.5-inch cover display, along with Touch ID instead of Face ID, an A20 chip, and Apple's C2 modem. The device is expected to be released alongside the i Phone 18 Pro models in September. Apple's book-style foldable could launch as the " i Phone Ultra ," as suggested by reports . Related Roundup: i Phone Fold Tags: Foldable i Phone , Nikkei This article, " Apple Ramps Foldable i Phone 'Ultra' Production to 10 Million Units " first appeared on Mac Rumors.com Discuss this article in our forums

title: Why California’s carbon manure math doesn’t add up
summary:
Here's a highly condensed bulleted list of crucial facts extracted from the raw text:

**Core Story / Main Event:**
- California's carbon offsetting program for cattle farmers is flawed, as it overstates emissions reductions and locks in more warming in the future.
- The program pays dairy farmers to turn methane from manure into natural gas, but this approach reduces short-term warming at the cost of increasing long-term warming.

**Important Names, Companies, or People:**
- Aaron Smith, a UC Berkeley economist
- California's air resources board

**Pricing, Release Dates, or Financial Numbers:**
- No specific pricing or financial numbers mentioned
- The program was extended beyond 2050 in 2024

**Product Specifications, Hardware Details, or Software Features:**
- Anaerobic digesters capture biogas from cattle manure and convert it into natural gas
- Biogas can be used to fuel vehicles or generate electricity in a power plant

**Direct Quotes:**
- "adding one average biogas-powered vehicle to the fleet would produce enough LCFS credits to cover the deficits incurred by 26 similar gasoline-powered vehicles" - Aaron Smith
- No other direct quotes mentioned
